chief_complaint: A 55-year-old female patient presented with severe chest tightness and shortness of breath.
history: The patient was treated symptomatically and placed under observation. A follow-up ECG revealed biphasic, inverted T waves in precordial leads V2-V5, consistent with Wellens Type A.
exam: Sinus rhythm, a heart rate of 84/minute, and no ST-segment changes were initially observed.
labs: ECG showed sinus rhythm, a heart rate of 84/minute, and biphasic, inverted T waves in precordial leads V2-V5. Coronary angiography demonstrated moderate (40%) diffuse narrowing of the proximal LAD, mild subocclusion of diagonal branches 1 and 2, 50% stenosis of the left circumflex artery, 50% stenosis of obtuse marginal branch 2, and significant (70%) mid-segment stenosis of the right coronary artery.
